order: Phase 5 passes the fixed trusted JSON protocol to Codex before the stdin envelope and returns any remaining contract failure to the nearest implementation loop without output rewriting.
Phase 6 records the actual GitHub merge method and Phase 7 activates only through exact protected lineage observation; the Runner cannot repair history, infer ancestry, or reuse unrelated CI.
Phase 5 uses the trusted delivery grammar and returns invalid Agent results to the nearest correction loop without sanitization or retry; Phase 6 begins only after the local aggregate owner passes.

A protected release update runs the complete second-candidate lifecycle regression locally before the remote release gate; the Runner cannot reuse an older enforced record or treat PR/main CI as a substitute for that preflight.
Runner phases consume resolved procedural guidance but cannot weaken AGENTS.MD invariants or reinterpret an invalid local instruction as absent.

These seven compatibility phases remain the fallback unless the exact repository has passed externally trusted, candidate-bound headless Activation; the paused Control Center is not an execution prerequisite and cannot grant release authority.

A protected runtime change must prove exact same-range parent document synchronization, environment-aware isolation refusal or real containment, the focused Next Workflow aggregate, and ordinary PR/main CI without treating skipped unavailable-environment execution as production evidence; live provider discovery additionally proves its default read-only-root/private-tmpfs executable binding on a prerequisite-complete host.

Bootstrap, installed-launcher integrity, injected provider-discovery, and development-selection regressions may use complete private non-executed provider descriptors on CI without Codex; release proof still requires the actual installed, pinned, certified, and observed native provider, and missing real containment explicitly skips positive bootstrap and launcher integrity while guided refusal passes.

Post-main installation and every Owner action require the same persistent checkout identity resolved by its owner; the wrapper pins the verified Controller base, and the runner cannot synthesize that ID from tracked repository configuration or treat a failed action as release proof.
The Runner may wait only through the protected runtime's bounded grace period for transient post-exit unknown identity; immediate matched/reused outcomes and persistent unknown remain fail-closed.

The Runner may invoke confirmed runtime reconcile only through the immutable external Owner Controller; it must not route Owner authority through the sanitized Production launcher or direct repository code.
The Runner may settle a terminal spawn effect only after protected lifecycle absence and persisted launch verification; it cannot accept the Agent result or authorize a retry.
